title: scirpus triqueter subsp. littoralis
intro: Scirpus triqueter subsp. littoralis, also known as Saltmarsh Bulrush, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, typically found in coastal areas and salt marshes. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Scirpus triqueter subsp. littoralis include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make medicine, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their effects.
